title: Symantec Endpoint Protection Client Scan Threat Findings
app: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection (Splunkbase 2772)
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:scan:file" earliest=-7d
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval scan_type=coalesce(Scan_Type, scan_type, "unknown")
| search infect* OR threat* OR virus*
| stats count by host, scan_type
| sort -count
description: Client scan logs that mention infections or threats reveal endpoints where on-demand or scheduled scans are still finding malware after real-time protection, often due to dormant files or policy gaps.
value: Prioritizes hosts needing deeper investigation when scans—not just real-time engines—report malicious content, supporting hunt and vulnerability management handoffs.
implementation: Ingest Client Scan log dumps into the SEP TA. Because scan log field names vary by SEPM version, validate extracted fields (`Scan_Type`, threat counters) in Search before production alerts; replace the inline `search` filter with explicit field thresholds when available. Exclude backup admin paths that routinely flag quarantine archives.
visualization: Table (host × scan type), timechart of matching scan events, single value (distinct hosts).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection
Symantec Endpoint Protection (SEPM) — SC4S sourcetype reference
